name: Cousous Topped with Almonds
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Carrots, peeled and sliced into thin rounds
1 large Bell pepper (green, red, or yellow)
1 cup Green beans, cut into 1-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
----------------------------------MARINADE----------------------------------
1/4 cup Canola oil
1/2 cup Orange juice
1/4 teaspoon Cinnamon
2 tablespoons Lemon juice
1/2 cup Fresh parsley, chopped
1 tablespoon Fresh dill, mixed
Place the couscous in a large bowl and stir in the boiling water. Cover and let sit for 10 to 15 minutes, stirring occasionally with a fork to fluff. Steam the carrots, pepper, and green beans until just tender (about 1-2 minutes for peppers, 2 minutes for green beans, and 4 minutes for carrots). Add the red onion, currants, and almonds to the couscous. Whisk together all ingredients for the marinade. Stir into the couscous mixture and let chill in a covered bowl in the refrigerator for 2 hours, or until completely cool. Do not leave the mixture out longer than this, as it will become mushy.
Food Exchange per serving: 1 STARCH/BREAD EXCHANGE + 1 FAT EXCHANGE (There is no specific vegetable exchange listed)
Calories: 155
Carbohydrates: 23g
Protein: 4g
Fat: 5g
Cholesterol: 8mg
Source: Diabetes Forecast; February 1994
